skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.summary: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified a shell command and filesystem write instruction at SKILL.md line 7. The command is a hardcoded mktemp invocation for a temporary Markdown handoff file, while the weak cryptography blocker findings at lines 3 and 13 are not supported by the file content.

low_findings.title: Controlled Temporary File Creation
low_findings.description: The instruction tells the agent to save a handoff document to a path produced by `mktemp -t handoff-XXXXXX.md`. This is a real external command and filesystem write pattern, but the command is hardcoded and does not include user-controlled shell input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact command and write target pattern are visible on line 7. Risk is limited because the command uses fixed arguments and writes to a temporary Markdown file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Not Supported
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm blockers at lines 3 and 13. Those lines contain descriptive text about creating a handoff and tailoring it to user arguments; no cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher, or crypto API evidence was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are plain-language skill instructions. They do not contain crypto-related code or algorithm names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Shell Command Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references `mktemp -t handoff-XXXXXX.md` to create a temporary file path. This is a low-risk command pattern because it is fixed and used only for local handoff document creation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command is explicitly present in the instruction. There is no evidence of dynamic command construction or command injection.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions lose context when work moves to a new agent. This skill creates a concise handoff document with references, next steps, and suggested skills.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the current conversation into a handoff document.
Saves the handoff as a temporary Markdown file.
Suggests relevant skills for the next session when appropriate.
References existing artifacts by path or URL instead of duplicating them.
Tailors the handoff to user-provided focus arguments.
limitations: It depends on the current conversation context available to the agent.
It does not complete the follow-up work described in the handoff.
It may include sensitive context unless the user states exclusions.
It cannot verify referenced artifacts unless the agent can access them.

use_cases.title: Continue Development Work
use_cases.description: Create a concise record of decisions, files, blockers, and next steps before a coding session ends.

use_cases.title: Transfer Research Context
use_cases.description: Summarize sources, open questions, and existing notes so another agent can resume investigation quickly.

use_cases.title: Preserve Project State
use_cases.description: Capture useful session context without copying full plans, issues, diffs, or design documents.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for the next session.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when you need a simple summary before ending a session.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff focused on the remaining implementation work.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when the next agent will continue a specific part of the project.

prompt_templates.title: Artifact-Aware Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff that references existing plans, diffs, issues, and commits by path or URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when important context already exists in project artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Skill Recommendation Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for the next session and include any skills that should be used.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when the next agent may need specialized workflow support.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff focused on finishing the review workflow.
output_examples.output: A temporary Markdown handoff that summarizes the current state, names the review workflow as the next focus, lists open decisions, and references existing artifacts.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff and suggest useful skills for the next session.
output_examples.output: A concise handoff document with the session summary, next actions, relevant artifact references, and suggested skills for the next agent.

best_practices: State the next session focus when you want a targeted handoff.
Keep plans, issues, and decisions in durable artifacts that the handoff can reference.
Ask the agent to omit sensitive details that should not be written to a temporary file.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for project documentation or issue tracking.
Do not ask it to duplicate full diffs, PRDs, plans, or commits.
Do not include secrets or private credentials in the conversation before creating a handoff.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Markdown handoff document that helps another agent continue the work.

faq.question: Where does the handoff file go?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to create a temporary file path with mktemp.

faq.question: Does it choose skills for the next agent?
faq.answer: It can suggest relevant skills when the current context indicates they may help.

faq.question: Does it copy every artifact into the handoff?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to reference existing artifacts by path or URL.

faq.question: Can I focus the handoff on one task?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass a short description of the next session focus as an argument.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk from local temporary file creation and no confirmed malicious behavior.
